name: Freshdesk MCP Server
what_it_does: Freshdesk MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Freshdesk's customer support platform — managing tickets, contacts, and companies; querying support history; automating ticket workflows; and integrating Freshdesk helpdesk data into agent-driven customer service automation.
use_cases: Creating and updating support tickets from customer service agents
Querying customer contact history and ticket status from support agents
Automating ticket routing and prioritization from ops agents
Searching Freshdesk knowledge base from self-service agents
Generating support analytics and SLA reports from reporting agents
Enriching support context with CRM data from integration agents
not_for: Teams using Zendesk, Intercom, or Salesforce Service Cloud as primary support platform
Enterprise support requiring Salesforce Service Cloud capabilities
best_when: An agent needs to automate Freshdesk helpdesk workflows — ticket management, customer lookup, or support queue management.
avoid_when: You're using Zendesk, Intercom, or another support platform.

auth.notes: Freshdesk API key (available in profile settings). Used with Basic auth as username, password is 'X'. Account-level access — no scope granularity. Per-agent API keys available.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 10 agents, basic ticketing features
pricing.notes: Generous free tier for small teams. API access included across all tiers. Freshworks suite offers additional products (CRM, ITSM). MCP server is community open source.

performance.rate_limits: 1000 req/min for Pro and Enterprise; lower for free tier

known_agent_gotchas: Subdomain required for all API calls — format: https://yourcompany.freshdesk.com
API key used as Basic auth username with 'X' as password — unusual auth pattern
Rate limits vary significantly by plan — free tier is very restrictive
Ticket field IDs differ per Freshdesk account — agents must discover schema dynamically
Community MCP server — endpoint coverage may be incomplete
Custom ticket fields have dynamic IDs that vary by account configuration
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key with Basic auth — full account access, no scopes. SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR. Community MCP server — audit before production use.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key via Basic auth — no scope granularity. SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR. Community MCP server.
